title: glechoma podostachys
intro: Glechoma podostachys, also known as Ground Ivy or Creeping Charlie, is a low-growing, perennial plant in the mint family with creeping stems and small, blue-purple flowers. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its compounds such as rosmarinic acid and glycosides. The health benefits of Glechoma podostachys include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce stress and anxiety, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in topical applications for skin issues and wounds.
